summary: Legacy mongos topology leaks servers not identified to be mongos instances
description: When the legacy mongos topology initially connects its seedlist, if it identifies any of the connected servers not to be mongos instances, it will remove it from the proxy list but leave the servers connected.

summary: The ignoreUndefined option does not apply to methods beginning with findOne
description: The ignoreUndefined option does not apply to methods beginning with findOne.

summary: Clean up functional tests to eliminate hanging with new test runner
description: As our test runner improves, we are noticing problems with our existing tests, especially with closing the client. In order to prove our test runner is working, we need to fix these issues with our functional tests.

summary: Remove mongodb-topology-manager dependencies
description: Once we can use mongo-orchestration for running the tests, we need to remove dependencies on mongodb-topology-manager and ensure that all the functions of mongodb-topology-manager are now being covered by mongo-orchestration.

summary: Find local mongo-orchestration
description: In order to support NODE-2045, we need to find the path to the local {{mongo-orchestration}}.

summary: Connections are leaked if pool is destroyed during initial connect
description: If a pool has an in-flight connection when its destroyed during its initial connect, the pool will destroy itself but neglect to destroy the connection (if it was successfully established).

summary: Sharded tests are marked `sharded` and `mongos` in tests
description: There are a number of sharded topology tests which are not being run because their required topology type is {{mongos}} instead of {{sharded}}

summary: useUnifiedTopology prevents connecting to replica set if secondary is down
Introduce ServerDescription equality to reduce SDAM events
description: The SDAM spec describes an algorithm for {{ServerDescription}} equality. SDAM events for description updates should be debounced by checking if incoming and existing descriptions are equal.

summary: support ability to pass hint to update
description: See DRIVERS-751 for details.

summary: Add SDAM test for debouncing topology description updates
description: See DRIVERS-760 for details.

summary: Legacy SDAM ReplSet topology reports incorrect topology ID
description: There is a small bug where the topologyId from the parent of  a {{Server}} is not saved, and subsequent SDAM monitoring events have incorrect values.

summary: Servers are not removed from unknownServer list, leaking
description: The legacy replset topology has a regression where connecting servers are not removed from the {{unknownServers}} list, leading to a leak on every topology update (>10s).

summary: Integrate a state machine into the unified topology
description: In order to prevent additional connections from occurring during a topology shutdown, the unified topology should use a state machine to track its current connection state.

summary: Enforce user bson options when using client-side encryption
description:
BSON has multiple options that dictate serialization behavior. The current default flags are:

{{bson.serialize}}
{code}
/**
 * Serialize a Javascript object.
 *
 * @param {Object} object the Javascript object to serialize.
 * @param {Boolean} [options.checkKeys] the serializer will check if keys are valid.
 * @param {Boolean} [options.serializeFunctions=false] serialize the javascript functions **(default:false)**.
 * @param {Boolean} [options.ignoreUndefined=true] ignore undefined fields **(default:true)**.
 * @param {Number} [options.minInternalBufferSize=1024*1024*17] minimum size of the internal temporary serialization buffer **(default:1024*1024*17)**.
 * @return {Buffer} returns the Buffer object containing the serialized object.
 * @api public
 */
{code}

{{bson.deserialize}}
{code}
/**
 * Deserialize data as BSON.
 *
 * @param {Buffer} buffer the buffer containing the serialized set of BSON documents.
 * @param {Object} [options.evalFunctions=false] evaluate functions in the BSON document scoped to the object deserialized.
 * @param {Object} [options.cacheFunctions=false] cache evaluated functions for reuse.
 * @param {Object} [options.cacheFunctionsCrc32=false] use a crc32 code for caching, otherwise use the string of the function.
 * @param {Object} [options.promoteLongs=true] when deserializing a Long will fit it into a Number if it's smaller than 53 bits
 * @param {Object} [options.promoteBuffers=false] when deserializing a Binary will return it as a node.js Buffer instance.
 * @param {Object} [options.promoteValues=false] when deserializing will promote BSON values to their Node.js closest equivalent types.
 * @param {Object} [options.fieldsAsRaw=null] allow to specify if there what fields we wish to return as unserialized raw buffer.
 * @param {Object} [options.bsonRegExp=false] return BSON regular expressions as BSONRegExp instances.
 * @return {Object} returns the deserialized Javascript Object.
 * @api public
 */
{code}

These fields are currently NOT respected when using Client Side Encryption. This is especially problematic for AutoEncryption, which is supposed to be a seamless experience with no noticeable difference in experience. The two main problems are:

1. If serialization options are not respected, then we run the risk of corrupting user data on inserts / updates, and corrupting queries when doing reads. This includes both encrypted fields *and* unencrypted fields that are on the same document as an encrypted field.
2. If deserialization options are not respected, there can be a degree of client-side data loss w.r.t. type promotion of various number types. For example a user passing {{promoteLongs: false}} is expecting to get back a {{Long}} object instead of a promoted {{number}}.
3. If deserialization options are not respected, it makes for a confusing API experience.
4. (minor) the lack of deserialization option respect is blocking NODE-2052

summary: Unified topology never regains nodes which temporarily go down
description: As shown in basic uses of the {{sdam_viz}} tool, the unified topology seems to have a serious bug where replset nodes which are lost are not rediscovered when they return to the cluster. This seems to be related to the monitoring loop being disabled since monitoring commands and normal commands both emit {{error}} events from the Pool. I also suspect this is highly related to users getting high degree cases of {{Server selection timed out}} messages, since selection will necessarily time out if no servers are ever available.

summary: Incorrect removal of Unknown server type from ReplicaSetNoPrimary topology
description:
I apologize if this is not a bug and merely a misunderstanding of the spec/implementation on my part, but I am hoping to at least get clarification on a possible inconsistency I have noticed while doing some chaos testing against a replicaSet.

According to [this table in the SDAM spec |[https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/server-discovery-and-monitoring/server-discovery-and-monitoring.rst#topologytype-table]], when a topology of type ReplicaSetNoPrimary receives a server of type Unknown the driver should update the description and do nothing. However, in [lib/core/sdam/topology_description.js#L192|#192]|[https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/next/lib/core/sdam/topology_description.js#L192]] it appears that the driver opts to remove Unknown servers from the topology description.

summary: Pick best format for boilerplate connect code
normalize state management across all stateful types
description: We have _tons_ of ways of tracking the state of types: {{Session.prototype.hasEnded}}, txns have state variables similar to pool, cursor has both internal state variables AND public accessors such as {{isDead}}. Let's come up with a solution that meets the need of all of our cases and standardize across all types.

summary: Make "cursorState" a public API to wait for ready streams
description:
When getting a {{stream}} from a {{collection.watch}}, it should be possible to access {{cursorState}} to wait for a {{stream}} to be ready.

 

My use case: I'm using WebSockets to let the client know when a specific collection change occurs. If I don't wait for the {{stream}} to be ready, it could happen that the client never gets informed about the collection change.

 

This resulted from a question at stackoverflow: [https://stackoverflow.com/questions/56699849/how-do-i-know-when-a-stream-node-js-mongodb-is-ready-for-changes/56708456#56708456]

summary: Missing arrayFilters method in bulk operations
description:
Acording to this issue: https://jira.mongodb.org/browse/NODE-1911 it should be fixed, but submitted PR just adds single test to `collection.updateMany` method in `test_bulk.js` file which is another issue.

Looks like `BulkOperationBase` class can handle `arrayFilters` via `raw` method: https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/master/lib/bulk/common.js#L880 but there is no `arrayFilters` method whatsoever.

summary: `useUnifiedTopology` continuously emits 'close' event when replica set member is down
MaxListenersExceededWarning caused by topologyDescriptionChanged
Fix idleShutdownTimeoutSecs
description: Not sure this is actually working.

summary: Document the design and rationale of the unified topology
description: It's unclear to some people why we would want to write the unified topology in the first place, as well as why some design decisions were made. We should document the rationale and general design of the unified topology.

summary: isConnected() takes legacy path with useUnifiedTopology
Unhandled error when server is offline
Correct client metadata for unified topology
description: The unified topology incorrectly identifies itself as being {{nodejs-core}}, this should change.

summary: remove outdated evergreen configurations
description: There are a number of evergreen configurations that are no longer supported by certain versions of node, and should be removed from our config.

summary: Only apply TransientTransactionError label within a transaction
Add driver API documentation for Field Level Encryption
description:
Currently the [node API docs|https://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/3.3/api/] do not have any references to the Field Level Encryption classes (that I can find).

Specifically neither {{ClientEncryption}} nor {{KeyVault}} appear to be defined. This may prevent users from utilizing manual encryption *or* from performing data key management.

Compare to the python docs where there is an [encryption|https://api.mongodb.com/python/current/api/pymongo/encryption.html] class.

There does exist documentation on the [{{AutoEncryptionOptions}}|https://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/3.3/api/global.html#AutoEncryptionOptions] {{MongoClient}} parameter.

summary: [FLE] Add prose test for explicit encryption with key alt name
Improve `./etc/build-static.sh` to work with any arbitrary URL for C Driver
description:
B/c of MONGOCRYPT-87, we need to update the version of `mongo-c-driver` we build against to a specific SHA. This caused more changes in the script b/c we did things like not force the name of the untar-d directory to be uniform.

We should modify the script such that any arbitrary url providing a tarball of mongo-c-driver can be used.

summary: Driver must error if autoEncryption is attempted against wire version < 8
Bulk write error returns incorrect index in WriteError for unordered writes
[FLE] Add a corpus test that exhaustively enumerates all ways to encrypt all BSON value types
description: See DRIVERS-686 for details

summary: Node Driver does not follow Initial DNS Seedlist Discovery spec
Server selection timed out
Enabling useUnifiedTopology results in stack overflow when connecting to uninitialized replica set
useUnifiedTopology - replicaset
Get a github token for publishing using `prebuild` that is not Dan's github token
description: Currently we are using my github token to auto-push the built distros for {{prebuild}}. We should get one on a special mongodb account not associated with any particular user.

summary: Document and notify on deprecated events and options for unified topology
description: The unified topology deprecates a number of events and supported options for {{MongoClient}}. We should warn users when they are using incompatible options, as well as when they try to listen for these events which will never be emitted.

summary: Replica set causes MongoNetworkError due to not connecting with fully qualified domain name
getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND error on Windows 10 due to failed fallback to ipv4 after ipv6 lookup fails
Unexpected behaviours using useUnifiedTopology: true
description:
Creating a MongoClient instance with useUnifiedTopology: true causes the following issues:
 # MongoClient.isConnected() always returns true, even if server got disconnected many seconds ago (even exceeding the reconnectTries*reconnectInterval period), but a subsequent query triggers an error: MongoError: server instance pool was destroyed).
 # No "timeout", "error", "reconnect", "reconnectFailed" events are triggered.

I need to detect when the MongoClient failed to reconnect (reconnectTries) times to the server to react accordingly (in this specific case, I just want to re-instantiate MongoClient after a timeout.

For me, using the topology monitoring events doesn't help/make sense  as well, as I need to detect this specific behaviour.

The only available option I have is to exit the node process abruptly in that case, to later restart it with something like pm2.

I don't really understand how these documented features are not properly working in any of the latest node-mongo-driver versions?

Thanks.

summary: Timed out connections should not half-close on destroy
description: A bug in the legacy connection type will attempt to half close a socket (send a FIN packet) in cases where the connection has already timed out due to an explicitly set socket timeout.

summary: Improve error message if the driver can't connect to mongocryptd
description: As a part of initialization for CSFLE, the driver will attempt to connect to a local {{mongocrypd}} using the default {{mongocryptdURI}} in 100ms. If the driver is unable to make this connection, it currently returns an unhelpful {{MongoTimeoutError: Server selection timed out after 100ms}}. We should return something more informative to the user.

summary: Support `idleShutdownTimeoutSecs` provided in both forms for mongocryptd options
description: {{mongocryptd}} supports {{--idleShutdownTimeoutSecs=VALUE}} as well as {{--idleShutdownTimeoutSecs VALUE}} as valid options. We need to support both styles.

summary: Allow connection establishment to be cancelled
description: Occasionally a connection pool is destroyed while connections are in-flight. Allowing connection creation to be cancelled would allow us to clean these up without waiting for the {{connectTimeoutMS}}

summary: Typo in `rescanIntervalSrvMS` causes spike in DNS lookups
